2603.18425.title: Multimodal Task Interference: A Benchmark and Analysis of History-Target Mismatch in Multimodal LLMs
2603.18425.paper_summary: Task interference, the performance degradation caused by task switches within a single conversation, has been studied exclusively in text-only settings despite the growing prevalence of multimodal dialogue systems. We introduce a benchmark for evaluating this phenomenon in multimodal LLMs, covering six tasks across text and vision with systematic variation of history-target along three axes: modality mismatch, reasoning mismatch, and answer format mismatch. Experiments on both open-weights and proprietary models reveal that task interference is highly directional: switching from text-only to image-based targets causes severe performance drops, while the reverse transition yields minimal degradation. Interference is further amplified when mismatches co-occur across multiple dimensions, and is driven most strongly by modality differences, followed by answer format, while reasoning requirement shifts cause minimal degradation.
2603.18425.paper_summary_zh: 任務干擾，即在單一對話中因任務切換而導致的表現下降，儘管多模態對話系統日益普及，但至今僅在純文本環境中進行研究。我們引入了一個基準，用於評估多模態 LLM 中這一現象，涵蓋六個文本和視覺任務，並在三個軸上系統性地變化歷史-目標：模態不匹配、推理不匹配和答案格式不匹配。在開放權重和專有模型上的實驗顯示，任務干擾具有高度的方向性：從純文本轉向基於圖像的目標會導致嚴重的性能下降，而相反的轉換則造成的降級最小。當多個維度同時出現不匹配時，干擾進一步加劇，且最強烈地受到模態差異的驅動，其次是答案格式，而推理要求的變化則造成的降級最小。
2603.18425.author: Masayuki Kawarada et.al.
2603.18425.authors: Masayuki Kawarada, Tatsuya Ishigaki, Hiroya Takamura
